def: adj. & s.m. Cowardly, timorous, timid, faint-hearted, shrinking, irresolute; gentle; — discouraged; distressed, agitated; confused, perplexed; — a cowardly or faint-hearted person, &c.; — timidity, &c. ( = kātaratva, q.v.); — a large kind of fish, Cyprinus catla.
more: adj. Timid, timorous, confused, distressed, agitated with distress.

def: s.m. A dagger with a broad straight blade, the hilt of which comes up on either side of the wrist, and which is grasped by a cross-bar in the centre; a dagger, poniard, stiletto, dirk
more: s. m. 1. ( कट्टार ) A dagger. 2. A polecat.
subs: s.m. One who carries a dagger, an assassin
or kaṭār mārnā (-ko), To put a dagger into (one), to stab.

def: s.m. A pole-cat ( = khaṭās, q.v.). s.f. Sourness, acidity ( = khaṭṭāpan, khaṭāʼī).

n. m. S. कट्टार A dagger; poniard; stilletto; dirk. ☞ A dagger having a broad straight blade the hilt of which comes up on either side of the wrist while it is grasped by a crossbar in the centre. 1. Karihaiyāṅ pe chale kaṭār, joban par tegā chale . Song. Her waist so slim makes falchions flash, o'er he/?/som's charms swords are drawn. 2. Koṭhe ūpar koṭhṛī sotī palaṅg bichhāë, Jad jāgūṅ jad ekli re! marūṅ kaṭārā khāë ! Dohā. Lone upon the housetop sleeping on my couch I lie, Lone I wake—ah! still alone! I'll stab myself and die. kaṭār bhoṅknā yā mārnā , v. a. To stab.
more: n. m. H. kū bad, ṭārī a horse. A hack.

def: n. m. The sound made in munching hard substances.
s.f. Cutting, clipping, &c. (used chiefly in comp.); — kattar or katar, s.f. A fillet
more: n. f. S. कृत् to cut. A fillet.

adj. S. कठोर Merciless; pitiless ( sakht , 4). Dard bachchī ko lage, kaise, ajī, ho be-kaṭar! Fāl khulvāte nahīṅ ho pās kar-ke māl kā! Jan Sāhab. The girl is in the throes, so hard-hearted you are, An omen you wont have, so close-fisted you are.
subs: s.m. pl. Artifices, wiles, tricks, feints, stratagems, manœuvres
s.m.(f.?) Cutting out (clothes); cutting and clipping; cuttings, deductions; thrift, parsimony; illicit picking, overcharge, peculation; — (met.) meditation; anxiety, disquiet; consultation
adv. By cutting and clipping; by careful management or contriving; by hook or by crook, &c.
(kā or -kī?) To cut out; to cut and clip, to retrench, to deduct (from); to pick and filch, to practise peculation, to purloin; — to plan, devise, contrive.

def: adj. & s.m. Hard, cruel, merciless, relentless; — a hard or cruel man; a caitiff.
s.m. The sound of crunching or munching grain, &c.; crunching, munching
subs: or kuṭur-kuṭur, s.m. Idem; — ady. Crunch-crunch, munch-munch.

ety: prob. S. कान्तारकः; but cf. kaṭārā
def: s.m. A variety of the sugar-cane; — tamarind.
more: W., n. m. 1. (E. kitārī ) A kind of sugarcane. 2. (E. kangal ) Tamarind. Nandī amal kaṭār saë, sāsar loh kaṭār, Pūr kapaṭ kī khān saë, kā sūṅ karūṅ pukār ? Rus wom. His sister is a tamarind sour, his mother a blade of steel, Himself a pit of deceit, dear! to whom can I appeal. 3. A medicinal plant; Globe thistle; Echinops echinatus . Roxb.
s. m. A kind of sugar-cane.

def: s.m. Name of a medicinal plant, the Globe-thistle, Echinops echinatus; — the aloe plant; — a kind of sugar-cane ( = katārā).
more: s. m. Name of a medicinal plant. (Globe thistle. Echinops echinatus. Roxb. ).

def: s.m. corr. of قطار qat̤ār, q.v. adj. Sufficient, enough (see faqat̤).
s.f. = S کاترتو कातरत्व kātaratva, s.m. Cowardice, timidity, timorousness, fear; agitation; dejection, depression of spirits.
s.m. = S کاترتا कातरता kātaratā, vulg. kātartā, s.f. Cowardice, timidity, timorousness, fear; agitation; dejection, depression of spirits.
n. Trickery; deceit; fraud.
s. (dakh.) See مکر makr.

s.m. See s.v. katar. adj. & s.m. Cowardly, timorous, timid, faint-hearted, shrinking, irresolute; gentle; — discouraged; distressed, agitated; confused, perplexed; — a cowardly or faint-hearted person, &c.; — timidity, &c. ( = kātaratva, q.v.); — a large kind of fish, Cyprinus catla.
more: n. f. 1. Cutting out (clothes). 2. Deductions; cuttings. 3. Overcharge; purloining; peculation. Yeh jo roz bāzār se saudā ātā hai is meṅ yeh nā-murādeṅ baṛī katar-biyoṅt kartī haiṅ . 4. ( Met .) Disquiet ( udheṛbun , 1—3). katar-biyoṅt se , adv. By cutting and clipping; by hook and by crook. Woh baṛe katar-biyoṅt se chaltā hai. katar-biyoṅt k. , v. a. 1. To cut out. 2. To cut; deduct; retrench. 3. To purloin; prig; filch; crib. 4. To plan; devise; contrive.
s. m. 1. Cutting out. 2. ( met. ) Meditation, consultation, anxiety.

def: s.f. A small dagger, a dirk, a stiletto; — a dagger-shaped figure (on cloth)
n. f. S. कट्टार A small dagger. kaṭārī mārnā , v. a. 1. To stab. 2. To afflict; injure.
s.m. A kind of silk cloth with stripes in the form of daggers (syn. kaṭārī-dār).
more: s. m. A kind of silk cloth with stripes in the form of daggers.

ety: katar˚ = S. कर्तय(ति), rt. कृत् 'to cut'
def: v.t. To cut, clip, pare, lop, prune, trim; to cut out; to cut up, to hew.
more: v. a. To clip; cut; cut out; pare; prune; cut up.
n. m. A kind of fish.
v. a. To clip, cut (as with scissors), cut out, pare, shred, cut up. ¶ kutarnā, v. a. To cut with the teeth.

def: s.m. A kind of fish.

ety: fem. of katarnā, q.v., or S. कर्तनी
def: s.f. Scissors; shears.
more: n. f. S. कर्त्तनी Pr. kattarī . Scissors. 1. Sūī, katarnī, gaj, uṅgleṭā, rākhe so darjī kā beṭā . Prov. saying. With whom you yard, thread, thimble, scissors see, a tailor's son, you may be sure, is he. 2. Chhurī, katarnī, ārsī, barchhī, aur tarvār, Apnā apnā kār deṅ apnī apnī bār . Rus.

def: s.f. Price paid for cutting out.
adj. Side; crooked, awry, zigzag; doubling (as a hare).
adj. Awry; bias (اُريب 1). katarvāṅ chāl , adj. 1. Zigzag; crooked. 2. Doubling (as a hare).
v.t. To cause to be cut or clipped, &c., to get or have (a thing) cut, or cut out, or trimmed, or pared, &c.
more: n. f. The price paid for cutting out.

def: n. m. A silk cloth with stripes in the form of daggers.
s.m. = khaṭās, q.v. s.f. Sourness, acidity ( = khaṭṭāpan, khaṭāʼī).
Tir. n. m. A polecat; wild cat.

ety: kāṭ(nā), q.v.+āsā = Prk. वंछओ = S. वाञ्छकः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Inclined to bite, addicted to biting; snappish; rabid ( = kaṭāhā).
adj. Cutting; biting; sharp, incisive, trenchant; caustic.
more: s. m. Excision, crop (of corn): jesting, mock, mockery, taunt. کٿاو کرنا kaṭāʼū karnā, To cut (corn), to taunt ( Harris ). ¶ adj. Cutting.

def: n. A contract. Das ser roz dūdh ek mahīne ke vāste kaṭaū kar līyā hai .

ety: kaṭāw(nā) = kaṭā(nā)+atī = Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आप्तिका
def: s.f. That which is to be de ducted, deduction; discount; exchange.

n. f. Discount. 1. Kaṭautī /?/ kar apnā rupayā le jāo! 2. Bhāī! aṛe pīsā rupaiyā kaṭautī kā kaṭe saë, kadhe pāchhe rāṛ kare . Rus. Friend! one pice on every rupee is taken here. I tell you beforehand, dont make a row afterwards.
adj. & s.m. = kaṭahā, q.v.
n. m. 1. Breach; cutting ( kat . 5). Pānī kā kaṭāo . 2. Embroidery.

ety: kaṭauwā, q.v.+al = Prk. अडो = S. लः
def: adj. = kaṭahā, or kaṭṭar, or kaṭkhanā, qq.v.
A thorny plant which bears a purple flower, Solanum jacquini (syn. gul-ḵẖār).
s.m. = kaṭh-bandhan, q.v.s.v. kaṭh.
s. m. A clog or ring of wood with which the feet of elephants are fastened.
part. adj. Cutting; cancelling, rescinding; incisive, keen, sharp; peremptory, decisive, explicit, categorical, conclusive, incontestible
more: adj. Incontestible; conclusive. qāta-i-safrā ; H. pit-haran , Antibilious.
part. act. (of قطع) 1. Cutting, rescinding. 2. adj. Peremptory, decisive, explicit. قاطع الطريق ḳāt̤iʼuﻌ-t̤-t̤arīḳ, A highwayman, a robber.

def: adv. (dialec., South. India), Saying; (much used as an expletive answering to) so that, so, thus, &c.

s.m. (dialec.) = kaṭharā or kaṭahrā, q.v. s.m. A large wooden cage; — a railing, a palisade; palings; a fence of wood; bars of wood; balustrade; banisters; frame-work (as at the end of a bed, or the sides of a cart, or around the sides of a ship or boat, &c.); — a block of wood on which fodder, &c. is cut.
more: s. (dakh.) Balustrade ( Harris ): see کٿهگهرا kaṭhgharā.

def: s.m. A cutter ( = kāṭne-wālā); one who cuts up, a butcher.

s.m. = kaṭh-gharā, kaṭhrā, qq.v. s.m. A large wooden cage; — a railing, a palisade; palings; a fence of wood; bars of wood; balustrade; banisters; frame-work (as at the end of a bed, or the sides of a cart, or around the sides of a ship or boat, &c.); — a block of wood on which fodder, &c. is cut.

s.m. = kaṭh-gharā, kaṭhrā, qq.v. s.m. A large wooden cage; — a railing, a palisade; palings; a fence of wood; bars of wood; balustrade; banisters; frame-work (as at the end of a bed, or the sides of a cart, or around the sides of a ship or boat, &c.); — a block of wood on which fodder, &c. is cut.
s.m. = qat̤-zan, q.v.s.v. qat̤t̤ or qat̤. adj. Sufficient, enough (see faqat̤).
s.m. Wood, timber; a piece of wood or timber, a log, stock, block; the stocks; — a scabbard
subs: s.f. A wooden doll, a puppet, a toy (more com. kaṭh-putlī, q.v.s.v. kaṭh)
lit. 'To chew wood'; to fare hardly or ill
or kāṭh-kā ullū, s.m. 'A wooden owl'; a great blockhead, an arrant fool; — adj. Impenetrable, blockhead of
s.m. A wooden horse; a lame man's stick, a crutch; a bier
s.m. Wooden articles (of furniture); a promiscuous heap of broken furniture; — stale, hard bread
s.f. A puppet; a very stupid woman; a ninny
s.f. lit. 'A wooden mare'; a bier; a coffin
s.f. A wooden pot; (met.) anything frail
(-ke), To put one's legs in the stocks
v.n. 'To become like a log,' to be or become petrified or paralyzed (with astonishment), to become stock-still (through fear, &c.).

def: s.m. Labour, pains, inconvenience, pain, suffering, hardship.
more: s. m. contract. of کاٿهہ kāṭh, q. v. کٿهہ بندهن kaṭh-bandhan, s. m. A wooden ring with which the feet of elephants are fastened. کٿهہ بروکي kaṭh-birūkī, s. f. A toad. کٿهہ بيلا kaṭh-belā, s. m. Name of a flower. (Jasminum multiflorum). کٿهپهوڙا kaṭh-phoṛā ( कुठार ) s. m. A woodpecker. (Picus). کٿهگلاب kaṭh-gulāb, s. m. A kind of rose. (Rosa chinensis). کٿهمستا kaṭh-mastā, adj. Very stout and lusty. کٿهہ هنسي kaṭh haṅsī, Affected or forced smiles (with internal displeasure).

def: n. m. Labour; pains. kaṭh karo to kām ho .
more: n. m. A wood from which a fast black dye is obtained. (?)

n. m. Cont. of کاتَهـ wood. kaṭh-bāp ; Sant. kākāt āpāt , n. m. A step-father. ☞ Women of the lower classes often leave, or are given up by their husbands, and on re-marrying ( karāo or dharāo ) they take with them the younger children to whom the new husband is kaṭh-bāp , while the children are called his ḍagarvā laṛke (picked up on the road or ḍagar ). kaṭh-birūkī, kath-meṅḍak , n. f. A toad. kaṭh-bandhan , n. m. A wooden ring to which the feet of elephants are fastened. kaṭh-bagṛā , n. m. A snipper; an ignorant tailor. kaṭh-baid , n. m. An ignorant practitioner; an empiric; a quack. kaṭh-bel ; Bhoj. chamarbelī , n. The acid wood-apple; Feronia elephantum. kaṭh-belā , n. m. Jasminum multiflorum. kaṭh-putlī , n. f. 1. A puppet; a toy. 2. A lean delicate girl. kaṭh putlī kā nāch , A puppet show; marionettes. kaṭh-phoṛā, khuṭak-baṛhaiyā, lakkaṛ phoṛā , W.; kaṭh-khudvā , E.; satraṅgyā , Br…

def: s. m. 1. Wood, timber, stock, block, a pair of stocks. 2. A scabbard. کاٿهـ پتلي kāṭh-putlī, s. f. A puppet, a toy. کاٿهـ چبانا kāṭh chabānā (lit. To chew wood) to fare hardly. کاٿهـ کا الو kāṭh kā ullū, 1. s. m. A sad blockhead. 2. adj. Impenetrable, blockhead. کاٿهـ کباڙ kāṭh kabāṛ, Wooden articles. کاٿهـ کي بهمبو kāṭh kī bhambo, s. f. A sad blockhead (woman). کاٿهـ کيڙا kāṭh-kīṛā, A bug. کاٿهـ مين پانودينا kāṭh meṅ pāṅw denā, To be imprisoned, to be in the stocks. کاٿهـ مينڐک kāṭh-menḍak, A toad. کاٿهـ هونا kāṭh honā, 1. To pine away. 2. To be petrified with astonishment.

n. m. S. काष्ठ, 1. Wood; timber. 1. Bhāi! kāṭh kī roṭī peṭ ke bāṅdhoṅ bhūk nā ṭale . Rus. Prov. (Said by the creditor). My dear fellow! my hunger is not to be appeased by a wooden plate in lieu of bread. 2. Are bole nā! kyoṅ kāṭh-kā ban gayā? Rus. What, cant you speak? What makes you as mute as a post? 2. The stocks. Bhāī! Rām ne berāṅ-kar kāṭh sūṅ pāoṅ nikāsā saë . Rus. Friend! By the grace of Ram my legs are out of the stocks. kāṭh , adj. 1. Hard. 2. Cruel. kāṭh chabānā , v. a. lit . to chew wood. To fare ill. kāṭh kā ullū, kāṭh-kā , n. m. An arrant fool. kāṭh kā ghoṛā , n. m. lit . a wooden horse. 1. A lame man's stick; a crutch. 2. A bier. 1. Laṅgaṛ-dīn paune tīn, kāṭh kā ghoṛā, lohe kā zīn . Boys. The lame man see, legs a quarter under three, a horse of wood, an iron saddle good! 2. Bhāī! kāṭh ke ghoṛe kitai na chalte dekhe . Rus. My good fellow! you never yet saw a wooden horse run. (Tell that to the marines). kāṭh kabāṛ , n. m. 1. A promiscuous heap of broken furniture, etc. 2. Stale hard bread. kāṭh kaṭavval , n. m. A boy's game. One boy who has hold of a post leaves it by way of defiance to another boy who takes the part of a thief and tries to catch him before he can return to the post. If he succeeds his captive becomes the theif. kāṭh kī bhambo , n. f. 1. A puppet. 2. A ninny. kāṭh kī ghoṛī , n. f. lit . a wooden mare. A bier; a coffin. kāṭh meṅ pāoṅ ṭhoknā yā denā , v. a. To put in the stocks. kyāṅṭh meṅ ṭhoke daroggā, kaiṅ ne phāṅsī, de divā, Le jī! ke bhāje haiṅ ham Hulsan kā tambū pāṛ ke? Rus. Dalmīr. The gaoler's put me in the stocks. may be he'll hang me next, Just see, my friends! have I Ṭhug. Wilson robbed, his tent cut through? kāṭh h. , v. a. lit . to become like a stick. To be petrified with astonishment; frozen. kāṭh kī hāṅḍī , n. f. 1. A wooden pot. 2. Anything frail.
s.m. (in comp.) Wood; (it sometimes expresses 'excellence,' 'superiority,' 'excess,' &c.)
subs: ( = S. maṇḍūkī), s.f. 'Wood-frog,' a toad; a lizard
s.m. 'A stick of a tailor,' a snip, an ignorant tailor
s.m. A wooden ring or clog by which the leg of an elephant is fastened
s.m. An ignorant practitioner, an empiric, a quack
s.m. The wood-apple, Feronia elephantum (syn. kapittha; kaith)
s.m. The large or double jasmine, Jasminum multiflorum, or J. hirsutum
or kaṭh-putrī, s.f. A wooden doll or image, a puppet; a toy; — a thin delicate girl; — kaṭh-putlī-kā nāć, s.m. A puppet-show; marionettes
(S. sphoṭakah), vulg. kaṭh-phāṛuʼā, s.m. A woodpecker, Picus
vulg. kaṭh-jīʼu, adj. & s.m. Hard-hearted, unmerciful; — hard-lived; — a hard-hearted man; — an animal that dies hard
s.m. A wooden frame over a well, &c.
s.m. The China rose, Rosa chinensis
s.f. Loud laugh, horse-laugh; affected or forced laugh (with internal displeasure).

ety: S. क्कथः, or क्काथः; and क्कथ+ कः
def: s.m. An astringent vegetable extract (the produce of the Mimosa catechu) which the natives of India eat with betel leaf; catechu, terra japonica (syn. khair, q.v.).
more: s. m. The astringent vegetable extract, which the natives eat with betel-leaf. It is the produce of a species of Mimosa (chadira) catechu, terra japonica.

def: s.f. A word; speech, discourse; a story, tale, relation, recital, narrative, fable
more: n. f. S. कथ to speak. 1. A narrative (قصه 1). 2. Preaching; religious recital. 1. Maṅgal karnī, Kal-mal harnī, Tulsī! kathā Raghunāth . Tulsī Dās. Joy-inspiring, Kaljug foulness cleansing, O Tulsi! is the story of Raghunāth. 2. Kari parinām nij kathā sunāī, Rām kripā āpan gatī pāī . Rāmāyan. 3. Bhāī! kisāṅṛ ne ke dhaule latte pahar-kar kathā bāṅchnī saë? Rus. My dear fellow! What has a peasant to do with clean clothes? Is he going to preach? kathā-bārtā , n. m. Religious controversy. kathā-bāṅchnā, bakhānnā, yā kahnā , v. a. 1. To read or recite a sacred book. 2. To preach. kathā bāṅchne-vālā , n. m. A preacher. kathā biṭhānā , Hin. v. a. To entertain a reader. kathā sampūran h. , v. n. To be finished (a kathā , q. v).
s. f. A story, tale, fable, relation, narrative.
subs: or kathā bakhānnā, To read or recite a story, or a sacred book, &c.; to preach
s.m. A composed story, a narrative, a literary composition or production, a tale, fiction
s.m. Talking, talk, conversation, speaking to or with; rumour, report
adv. In the form of words, by means of speech, in verbal form
s.m., or kathā-vārttā, s.f. Speech, discourse; controversy; intercourse; news, report, rumour
(˚thā+upa˚), s.m. Conversation, intercourse, conference, dialogue, narration.

def: n. m. A circular piece of iron placed on a ploughshare to prevent its going too deep into the ground.
s.f. (dialec.) The plant Solanum jacquini ( = kaṭāʼī).
adj. Of or relating to wood; wooden; — s.m. Name of a particular Hindū religious book.
s.m. Narrator, relater, reciter; one who publicly reads and expounds the Purāṇas; — a professional story-teller; a kind of singer or bard; chief actor; a dancing boy.
more: n. S. कथक 1. A singer. 2. A dancing boy. 1. Batānā Katthakoṅ par khatam hai . In the art of representation the Katthak is perfection. 2. Nāchoṅ meṅ nāch Katthak kā! Prov. The Katthak's is the dance to see!
s. m. 1. (for कथक r. कथ Speak) A storyteller. 2. (for कत्थक r. कत्थ Applaud) A kind of singer.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कथक (kathaka, “narrator”).

def: s.m. The Indian bread-fruit tree or jack, and its fruit, Artocarpus integrifolia.
more: s. m. Name of a fruit and tree. The jack tree and fruit. (Artocarpus integrifolia: Roxb. ).

def: n. m. S. कण्टकिफल The jack tree or fruit; Atrocarpus integrifolia , Wat. Kaṭhal hai yā jaṅglī chūhā? Phabtī . Jest. Is this a jack or a hedgehog?
s.m. The act of saying, or telling, narrating, informing; a saying, narration, relation, account, explanation; a composition; a byeword.
more: n. m. 1. A saying. 2. A byeword. 3. A composition; poetry.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कथन (kathana).

def: s.m. A short tale; a tale, story, fable.
s.m. Hardness of the stomach or abdomen; wind-dropsy; dropsy; tympany.
part. adj. To be said, to be told or declared; requiring to be said; worthy of relation; admitting of being uttered, fit to be uttered or said, to be named.
s.m. A paling; banisters, &c. ( = kaṭahrā, q.v.).
adj. Hard (cf. kaṭar or kaṭṭar; and kaṭhor).
more: 1. (from کٿهگهرا kaṭhgharā, q. v.) s. m. A wooden cage, in which wild beasts are kept. 2. See کٿہل kaṭʼhal.

def: s.m. = next, q.v.
(from کٿهگهرا kaṭhgharā, q. v. Sans. काष्ठगृह ) s. m. 1. A wooden cage. 2. A railing, a palisade. ¶ kaṭhrā, 1. (from काष्ठ Wood) s. m. A tub, tray, plate, platter, trough. 2. A young male buffalo.

s.m. A large wooden cage; — a railing, a palisade; palings; a fence of wood; bars of wood; balustrade; banisters; frame-work (as at the end of a bed, or the sides of a cart, or around the sides of a ship or boat, &c.); — a block of wood on which fodder, &c. is cut.
s.f. A wooden water-pot with a spout (syn. kamanḍal).
A wooden bowl, or dish, or cup; a small tub; a trough; a kneading-trough; a trencher, platter, plate.

n. f. A wooden or stone vessel for kneading flour; a kneading trough; a small tub. 1. Rāṅpī, ārī, suī kaṭhautī, yehī chamāran kī saë roṭī. Jaë hai Gurū Rāë Dās kī! Chisel, needle. awl, and wooden cup the cobbler help to breakfast, dine, and sup. Glory be to Gurū Rāë Dās! (The cobbler's morning prayer before he sets to work). 2. Ek to mīṭh, dūsar kaṭhaut bhar . Sweet, and a bucket full! 3. Ath kaṭhautī māṭṭhā pīyeṅ, solah makunī khāeṅ Unke mare na roiye, ki ghar ke diliddar jāëṅ! Prov. A general massacre. qatl-i-amad . Wilful murder; wilful homicide by an adult and sane person; homicide with premeditated malice. qatl qābil-i-sazā yā mustalzim-i-sazā . ety: kāt˚ = Prk. कत्त(इ) or कत्ते(इ); S. कर्तय(ति), caus. of rt. कृत् 'to spin'
def: v.t. To spin (cotton or thread); to twist or wrap round.
more: v. a. S. कर्त्तन To spin (thread). 1. Āgre kā chaṛkhā, Burhānpur kī rūī; Saiyāṅ gaë pardes, maiṅ to kāt kāt mūī . An Agra spinning wheel, from Barhanpore my cotton rare, My husband still away, I'm worn to death with spinning e'er. 2. Mat jā, pitam, bides tū, kanthā, hāṭ na khol, Hunar dekh mere hāth kā, kātūṅ sūt amol! Play. Go not, love, to foreign lands! Seek not gold by trade to win! See how deftly ply my hands! See what priceless thread I spin! 3. Kāt sūt, bahuaṛ! tauṅh nānhā, Sughaṛ kahe jo sagaṛ jamānā . Rus. My daughter-in-law! do you spin fine, 'fore all the world for skill you 'll shine. 4. Pīsnā na kātnā, baiṭhe gaṛaṅg ( bāṛang ) hāṅknā! Wom. She 'll neither grind nor spin, but sit and jaw and make a din. 5. Kātā aur le dauṛī! Wom. Prov. A handful spun, quick to the market run! nannhā kātnā , v. n. 1. To spin fine (thread). 2. To use sparingly; to be thrifty. Arī gharsī! isā nā…

v. a. S. कर्तन Pr. kaṭṭanam . See chhāṅṭnā , 1, 2, 5, 11, 12, alag k. , 1, 2, 4, 5, 6, toṛnā 2, 3, 10, 13, 16, 18). 1. To disjoin; disunite; divide; tear asunder; sever. 1. Woh kāṭā! (A Kite flying). There he's cut! 2. Jis ṭahnī par baiṭhe usī ko kāṭe . Prov. He cuts away the branch which supports him. (It is a foul bird that defiles its own nest). 2. To saw; cleave; fell; laminate; slice. 3. To settle a dispute. Rāṅd kāṭnā . 4. To open; lay out; make. Saṛak kāṭnā . 5. To clear (land). Jangal kāṭnā . 6. To dissect; anatomize. 7. To maim: mutilate. 8. To mow; reap. 1. Pahle bo, pahle kāṭ . Prov. Early sow, early mow. 2. Devegā so pāvegā, bovegā so kā/?/egā . Prov. Who give receive, who sow shall reap. 9. To carve; chisel ( tarāshnā , 2). 10. To deduct; discount. Mitī kāṭnā . 11. To draw a pen across; strike out; erase ( tarāsh kharāsh k . 1). 12. To cancel; nullify. 13. To refute. Dalīl k…

ety: kāṭ˚ = Prk. कट्ट(इ) or कट्टे(इ); S. कर्तय(ति), caus. of rt. कृत्
def: v.t. To cut, clip, lop, prune; to hack, slash, cut asunder, sever, disjoin, disunite, divide; to dissect; to saw; to cleave; to carve, to chisel; to mow, to reap; to clear (land of jungle); to bite; to nip, to sting; to cut or wound with satire; to carp at, find fault with; to make ashamed, to shame, to put to the blush, to confuse; to cut away, to erode; to corrode; to cut out, mark or lay out (a road); to cut out, strike out, erase; to cancel; to exclude; to pass, spend, or while away (time); to waste (time); to tarry, stop, stay, remain; to traverse, or cross, or get over (a road); to extract (colour from anything); to interrupt, or intercept (speech); to refute, rebut; to put an end to, settle, or adjust (a dispute, &c.); to deduct; to liberate; to earn, or gain (money)
more: v. a. 1. To cut, to clip. 2. To bite, corrode, erode. 3. To reap. 4. To saw. 5. To stop, to stay. 6. To waste, to spend, to pass away (the time), to pass (a road). 7. To interrupt, to intercept. 8. To shame, to make ashamed. کاٿ ڐالنا kāṭ ḍālnā, To cut off, amputate.
subs: (-ko), To cut off; to bite off
v.t. (intens.) To cut off, lop off, strike off, chop off, amputate; to cut down, to hew, &c.
v.t. (intens.), To cut off; to bite off, &c.
ex: میں بعد میں تربوز کاٹ دونگا۔
ma͠i bād mẽ tarbūz kāṭ dūṅga.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀝𑁆𑀝𑀇 (kaṭṭaï), from Sanskrit कर्त॑ति (kártati, “to cut”), from कृ॒न्तति॑ (kṛntáti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *kr̥ntáti.

def: v.n. To be spun.
more: v. n. S. कर्तन To be spun.

v. n. S. कृत cut. 1. See kāṭnā v. a. Sīr baṭā aur jhagṛā kaṭā . Rus. Partition made, quarrels stayed. 2. To be lavished, squandered. Unkā baṛā rupayā kaṭ rahā hai . 3. To be taken from one; to be eased of, fleeced. Kal āṭh rupaë ko kaṭ gaë . 4. To be remitted or forgiven (sins). Pāp kaṭnā . 5. To thaw; melt. Barf kaṭnā . 6. To dodge; slink away. Mujhe dekhte hī voh kaṭ-kar chalā gayā . 7. To be ashamed, put to shame. 8. To be jealous; to be jealous of. Jab unke barābar baiṭhte haiṅ, to dushman apne dil meṅ kaṭte haiṅ. kaṭ-chhālī , n. f. Tyre made of milk of which the cream has been skimmed. kaṭtī kahnā ; Rus. kaṭvāṅ kahnā , v. a. To speak against one. Lekh likhe to, bālke! meṭ na sakke ko, Lākh bairī kaṭvāṅ kaho, mastak līkhā so ho . Rus. What fate hath writ erased can never be, Whate'er foes lakhs may say what's writ will be.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀬 (kittiya) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀦- (-na-), from Sanskrit *कियत्त (*kiyatta), variant of कियत् (kíyat). Doublet of کِتَّا (kittā). Cognate with Punjabi ਕਿਤਨਾ /کِتْنا (kitnā).

def: v.n. To be cut, be clipped; to be reaped; to be cut off, be amputated; to be killed (in fight); — to be retrenched, to be diminished; to be deducted (from); to be spent, to be squandered; to be spent or passed (as time, life, &c.); to cease, to come to an end, be put an end to; to be interrupted; to pass away; to disappear, vanish; to dissolve, melt (as snow, ice, &c.); to be wiped or blotted out, be effaced or obliterated, be forgiven (as sins); — to come out, to be extracted (as colour from cloth, &c.); — to be ashamed, be abashed; to be consumed with jealousy, &c.
more: v. n. 1. To be cut, to be clipped or cut off. 2. To be spent or passed (time, life, &c.). 3. To be abashed, to be interrupted. 4. To die of wounds.
subs: v.n. To be cut off, be severed or sundered, be intercepted, be divided, be separated (from); to be stopped up (as a road infested with robbers, &c.); — to be spent, be consumed, be squandered, &c.
To cut and run, to slink away.

n. f. S. कुट्टनी A procuress; conciliatrix . 1. kuṭnī, naṭnī, kūṅjṛī rākheṅ nāṅh prīt, Dhokā inkī pīt māṅ jaise gauras sīt . Rus. The house of a procuress where clandestine prostitution is carried on. 2. A bawdy-house; a bagnio; brothel; a house of call.

n. m. Dim. kaṭorī , n. f. 1. A cup, etc. of metal. 1. Māmāṅ lāyā sāt kaṭorī, ek kaṭorī phūṭī, māmāṅ kī taṅg ṭūṭī . Lullaby. Lori . 2. Bharā kaṭorā dūdh kā maiṅ jholūṅ, tū pī! 2. A full-blown flower. ety: S. क्कथः, or क्काथः; and क्कथ+ कः
def: s.m. An astringent vegetable extract (the produce of the Mimosa catechu) which the natives of India eat with betel leaf; catechu, terra japonica (syn. khair, q.v.).
more: n. m. S. खदिर Catechu. An astringent vegetable extract which the natives eat with betel leaf; terra japonica . It is the produce of the Mimosa Catechu. 1. Katthā bhī rove, katarnī bhī, sarautā bujhāve sān, tamolan gaune chalī . The catechu weeps, her scissors too weep, her nut-crackers give the sign, the tobacconist to her husband's nuptial couch is gone. 2. Tū chūn-na * kar! maiṅ terā yār kat-thā † ? Tere rog ne mujhe chhā-līyā, ‡ chal baṅgle ¶ meṅ, mujhe le so-piyārī! § . Zila . Conundrum. Scream not! was ever I your lover, pray? Your grief's my shroud, home, love! to bed, I say! dūdhiyā katthā , White catechu.

def: s.m. A star (or stars) of gold, silver, or tinsel, &c. (worn as an ornament or insignia of rank).

ety: inf. n. of قول 'to speak; to say,' &c.
def: s.m. Speech, word, saying, dictum; assertion, declaration, affirmation; promise, agreement, stipulation, compact, consent, acquiescence; vow, resolve; — a kind of song
more: n. m. See بچن and اقرار 1, 2, 3. 1. Speech. 1. Mardoṅ kā ek qual hotā hai . Prov. A man will never go back from his word. 2. Qaul-i-mardāṅ jāṅ dārad . P. Prov. A man's words carry weight. 2. A quotation. Kisī kā qaul hai . 3. Allegation. 4. Stipulation ( bachan , 3). 5. Saying; text; motto. Qaul gā lo! 6. A resolve; vow. Hamārā to qaul hai. qaul toṛnā , v. a. To break one's word. qaul denā , v. a. See bachan denā . 1. Raṅgīṅ mujhe qaul de-ke, guiyāṅ! Kahne lagi 'lā na jī meṅ vasvās, Jo tujh se dagā kare to phir bas Ho us bandī kā sattiyānās!' Wom. Rangīn. 2. Nahīṅ voh qaul kā sachchā, hameshah qaul de de-kar Agar hāth us ne mere hāth par mārā tau kyā mārā? Zauq. To her word she's never true, though vow on vow she make Striking her hand on mine, it's only made to break. qaul se phirnā , v. n. To break one's word or promise; go back from one's word. qaul qarār , n. m. Mutual agreement; con…

(Heb. ) s. m. 1. A word, saying, assertion. 2. Agreement, promise, consent, contract, affirmation. 3. A kind of song. قول قرار ḳaul-ḳarār, s. m. Agreement. compact. قول قرار کرنا ḳaul ḳarār karnā, To covenant, to stipulate. قول قلبا نه ḳaul-ḳalbāna, A kind of song. قولنامه ḳaul-nāma, s. m. A written agreement, articles (of peace, &c.). قول وفعل ḳaul-o fiﻌl, s. m. Word and deed.
subs: (-ko), To pledge (one's) word (to); to promise; to vow, &c. (syn. baćan denā)
(with gen.), To go back from (one's) word, to break one's word or promise
s.m. Mutual agreement, compact, contract, bargain, convention, treaty, league
(-se), To make an agreement or compact (with), to stipulate, covenant; to make terms (with); to bind by contract
or qaul-kā saććā, adj. True to (one's) word, faithful to an engagement, &c.
s.m. A finger-ring representing two hands joined together
(-se), To pledge one's word (to); to make an agreement (with), &c. ( = qaul-qarār karnā)
(-kā), To take (one's) promise; to obtain the promise or consent, &c. (of)
s.m. A written agreement; a treaty, articles (of peace, &c.; — syn. iqrār-nāma, q.v.)
(-ko), To pledge (one's) word (to), to promise; to agree; to make terms; — to break (one's) word or promise
s.m. A simple declaration or affirmation; mere word or words
(-ke), According to the saying or dictum (of).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَول (qawl), from Arabic قَوْل (qawl).

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Belonging or relating to a family, ancestral; — sprung from a high or noble family, of good family, well-born (syn. kulīn); — a man of good family, &c.
s.m. The breast, bosom; lap; embrace; — cavity, hollow, niche, recess, corner or angle (of a room, &c.); a side wall; — an armful, as much as can be embraced in the two arms (see kaulī).
more: v. n. S. कूल The side walls. Aule re kaule guṛiyāṅ chhoṛīṅ, Maiṅ ne chhoṛā bābal kā des rī . Marriage song. Maṅḍhā . My dolls in niches put away, from my fatherland a waif astray. kaule silānā , v. n. To cool the walls on either side of a door (to keep off evil). kaule se lag-ke khaṛā ho jānā . To stand against the sidewall in anger or grief.
s. m. 1. ( कूल ) Corner (of a room, &c.). 2. ( क्रोड ) Embrace, bosom, armful. 3. ( कमला ) s. m. A kind of orange.

def: s.f. Bosom; lap; embrace, grasp of the arms; armful, as much (of anything) as can be grasped in the fold of the arms; a bundle or sheaf of corn given as a perquisite to a reaper or a village servant
more: n. f. 1. Grasp; hug; embrace. 2. As much as can be taken up between the two arms. Ek kaulī lakṛiyāṅ uṭhā lāo! kaulī bharnā , v. a. 1. To embrace. 2. To enfold between the two arms. 3. To be greedy ( hirs k .). Miyāṅ! kyoṅ āsmān kī kaulī bharte ho?
s. f. Embrace, grasp of the arms. See کولا kaulā, sign. 2.
subs: (-kī), To take or fold (one) in the arms, to embrace; — to be grasping or greedy.

s.f. A small mouthful, a morsel.

The interest of the nation. qaumī hukūmat . 1. Parliamentary Government. 2. A republic. qaumī zulm . Race oppression. qaumī majlis . A national assembly.
s. f. Tribe, sect, cast, a people, nation, family. قوم و خويش ḳaum-o-ḵẖẉesh, plur. m. Friends and relations.

def: s.f. corr. of qaum, q.v. s.f. A people, nation; a tribe, race, family; sect, caste

s.f. One of the seven divine Mātr̤is or Mothers, or personified energies of the gods; the śakti or energy of Kārttikeya, the god of war.
s.f. corr. of kumārī, q.v. s.f. A young girl (one from ten to twelve years old, which is regarded as a marriageable age), maiden, maid, virgin, damsel, lass; a daughter; a princess (see kuṅwārī)
subs: s.f. An unmarried damsel, a maid, virgin, &c. ( = kumārī)
s.f. The murder of a young girl.

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

def: (dialec.), 1˚ = ko (the case-sign of the dat. & acc., q.v.); — 2˚ = kā (the case-sign of the gen.); — 3˚ corr. of kaun, q.v.
more: from S. किम or के who, Pr. ke , Pālī, kaṭamo ko , Who; which; whom; what. Kaun jhagṛe meṅ paṛe?
adj. ( Brokers ) Nine. kaun āne iklā . One Rupee and nine annas.
pron. Who? which? what? whether? کون سا kaun-sā, Which? what? what sort of? ¶ koṅ, kūṅ, or kauṅ, postpos. denoting the objective case, To, for.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

ety: Ap. Prk. कवणु, or कवडु; Prk. केद्दहो; S. कीदृशः
def: pron. Who? which? what? whether? (the formative of the sing. is kis; that of the plur. kin or kinh; but in Hindī poetry, in Braj, and in rustic speech kaun is seldom inflected; thus we find kaun-kau instead of kis-kau ( = kis-kā), &c.)
subs: Which? what? which (or what) particular one? what sort or manner of? — kaun-hī (Braj. kawan-hī, or kawan-hū), emphat forms of kaun (see hī).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Apabhramsa कवण (kavaṇa), from Sanskrit कः पुनर् (kaḥ punar, “who”). Cognate with Assamese কোন (kün), Gujarati કોણ (koṇ), Marathi कोण (koṇ).

more: s. dual (of کون kaun ) The two worlds (the spiritual and corporeal, or that of angels and that of men).

n. f. S. कुण्ड pot. A tub or bucket.

def: s.m. = H کوند कौंद kauṅd, s.f. = kauṅdh and kauṅdhā, qq.v.
more: n. m. 1. A shallow earthen vessel; a kneading trough. Postī gayā ros, mahīne meṅ chalā ek kos . Ans. Woh koī qāsid hogā! yār to kūṅḍe ke is pār, yā us pār . An opium-eater one did rile, in one month he did walk a mile. Ans. 'He must be a postboy truly, I am on this side of the trough or on that.' ☞ The above saying is ascribed to a sot who never left the neighbourhood of his pottle. 2. ( Mah .) An offering made to a Saint. kūṅḍā k. , v. a. 1. To make an offering to a Saint. Hamsāī mere! sar kī qasam, āīyo zurūr, Kūṅḍā karūṅgī Jumme ko Saiyad Jalāl kā . Wom. Jān Sāhab. 2. Dicitur cum plures virī, unus post alterum, mulierem violant ( ardalī utarnā ).

ety: S. चक्षु?+अन्ध or अन्धी; of. ćauṅdhī
def: s.f. Flash (of lightning, &c.); brightness, brilliance, splendour, dazzling light.
more: (from کوندهنا) s. f. Splendour, brightness, flash (as, of lightning).

def: s.m. Lightning.
v.n. To flash, to glitter; to lighten
more: v. n. To lighten, to flash.

def: v.n. = kauṅdhnā, q.v. v.n. To flash, to glitter; to lighten
adj. Existent; material; worldly.

adj. (dakh.) Tender. کونلي هڐي kauṅlī haḍḍī (dakh.) Gristle, cartilage ( Harris ).

def: The world.
The world.
pron. See s.v. kaun. (dialec.), 1˚ = ko (the case-sign of the dat. & acc., q.v.); — 2˚ = kā (the case-sign of the gen.); — 3˚ corr. of kaun, q.v.

def: s.f. Counsel; council
more: n. f. Cor. of Counsel. 1. Kyā kauṅsal ho rahī hai, sāhab ? 2. Kar kaunsal Angrej kaid sab hudde-dār karāī, Bigṛe sipāhī, dhūm machāī, sab hudde-dār chhutāī, Fauj Dillī ko dhāī . Song. Holī. Mutiny of 1857. The English held a council and the Officers confined, The Sepoys turned and made a row and set them free, The Army all then quickly made straight for Delhī. kauṅsal k. , v. a. 1. See salāh k . 2. To plot; intrigue. kauṅslī, kauslī , n. m. A counsellor; barrister.
subs: To consult together, to hold a consultation; — to plot, intrigue.

def: s.m. Counsellor; a barrister.
s. f. See کنتي kuntī.
